agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver renews 2STGD+ Safety and Inclusion Action Plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the renewed 2STGD+ Safety and Inclusion Action Plan, which includes 30 city-wide actions for 2026–early 2027 across five goals: Housing & Homelessness, Community Spaces & Safety, Programs & Services, Visibility & Engagement, and Human Resources & Staff Experience. If approved, staff will report back annually with implementation updates and proposed future-year actions. The plan was informed by over 800 participants consulted across two engagement phases.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on February 24, 2026 — the plan may introduce new guidelines for business interactions with 2STGD+ customers and employees.

key_dates.label: Council vote on 2STGD+ Safety and Inclusion Action Plan

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver council to vote on BIA renewals for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on February 24, 2026 to renew seven BIAs expiring March 31, 2026. Six BIAs (Dunbar Village, Hastings Crossing, Kerrisdale, Kitsilano 4th Avenue, Mount Pleasant, West Broadway) seek 5-year renewals; West End (Davie Village) seeks a 10-year renewal. Objections did not exceed the 50% threshold needed to block renewal. If approved, by-laws will set new funding ceilings and continue annual fees for businesses in each BIA.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council's decision on February 24, 2026 — if you own a business in one of these BIAs, the renewal will affect your annual fees.
agenda_briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Landlord & Property Mgmt

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Closure and sale of road portion at 1710-1730 East Pender Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to close and sell a 57.2 sq m (616 sq ft) portion of road next to 1710-1730 East Pender Street for $280,000. If approved, the adjacent property owner would buy the land to consolidate it with their site for an 18-storey mixed-use building with 191 social housing units and commercial/retail at grade. The owner would also pay a $14,360 road closure fee and assume ownership of existing sewer connections within the road portion.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the February 24, 2026 council meeting and consider attending or submitting comments if you own or operate a business on East Pender Street.

key_dates.label: Council meeting to consider road closure and sale

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Street name changes for Marpole Transit and St. Paul's Hospital
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw to amend the Street Name By-law (No. 4054) to officially name new street extensions created as part of the Marpole Transit Centre Development and the new St. Paul's Hospital Development. This is a routine step to finalize addresses for new roads in these major projects. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 4.	A By-law to amend Street Name By-law No. 4054 regarding the naming of various street extensions in the Marpole Transit Centre Development and New St. Paul’s Hospital Development
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — February 24, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Special traffic lanes for FIFA World Cup 26 in Vancouver
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to allow FIFA-accredited vehicles to travel (but not stop) in existing special use lanes from June 11 to July 19, 2026. Affected street segments within 800m of provincial highways include Granville St (SW Marine Dr to 8450 Granville St), W 41st Av (Dunbar St to Crown St), W Georgia St (Chilco St to Broughton St north side; Denman St to Broughton St south side), Pender St (Nicola St to W Georgia St), E Hastings St (Boundary Rd to Slocan St), and McGill St (Hwy 1 on-ramps to Renfrew St). Transit and cycling movements are expected to remain largely unaffected.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council decision on February 24, 2026 — this could affect delivery schedules and customer access near designated routes.

key_dates.label: Special use lane designation period — FIFA World Cup 26

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Land use change for former Molson Brewery industrial site
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed land use change for the former Molson Brewery site (1550 Burrard Street and 1655 West 1st Avenue), currently designated as an 'Exceptional Industrial Site.' A proposed bylaw would re-designate the site from 'Industrial/Employment' to 'Mixed-Use High-Rise 2' under the Vancouver Official Development Plan, potentially allowing housing or commercial development. Staff also seek direction to initiate a Type 3 amendment to Metro Vancouver's Regional Growth Strategy to re-designate the site from 'Industrial' to 'General Urban.' The decision is not final — council is set to vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect future development or property values in the area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver Fire Bylaw changes on appointments
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw that would amend the existing Fire By-law No. 14419. The change deals with how certain appointments are made within the fire department. This is a routine administrative update, but it could affect how fire safety roles are filled. The proposal is up for a vote at an upcoming council meeting and has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.	A By-law to amend Fire By-law No. 14419 regarding appointments

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Housing agreement for 1302-1318 E 12th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw to enact a Housing Agreement for the property at 1302-1318 E 12th Avenue. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in certain conditions — in this case, it would likely secure affordable housing or rental terms for the units. This is not yet approved; council is set to vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 8.	A By-law to enact a Housing Agreement for 1302 – 1318 E 12th Avenue

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Housing agreement for 6486 Chester Street, Vancouver
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw to enact a Housing Agreement for the property at 6486 Chester Street. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in the number of affordable rental units and sets rules for how long they must stay affordable. This is not yet approved — council will vote on it at an upcoming meeting.

This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved. The vote is upcoming.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Housing agreement for 2110 West 5th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create a Housing Agreement for the property at 2110 West 5th Avenue. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in certain conditions — like how many rental units must be kept affordable and for how long. This bylaw has not been approved yet; council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect rental housing rules in your neighbourhood.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver council to amend housing agreement for 3 downtown sites
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend an existing Housing Agreement for three properties: 625 Pacific Street, 777 Pacific Street, and 1390 Granville Street (which includes Sub-Area C at 675 Pacific Street and 1395 Rolston Street). A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in how many affordable or below-market rental units must be provided. This amendment would change the terms of that agreement — for example, it could adjust the number of affordable units, the rent levels, or the length of time they must stay affordable. The decision has not been made yet; council will consider and vote on the bylaw at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date — if you own or manage these properties, attend or submit comments before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 13.	A By-law to authorize the amendment of a Housing Agreement Authorized by By-law No. 14225 regarding 625 Pacific Street, 777 Pacific Street and 1390 Granville Street (Sub-Area C - 675 Pacific Street and 1395 Rolston Street)

A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in affordable rental rates or other housing conditions for a set period. This is not yet approved — the bylaw is up for a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this agreement could affect future use or development of the property.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Housing agreement for 1525 Robson Street up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create a housing agreement for the property at 1525 Robson Street. A housing agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in certain conditions — like how many rental units must be kept affordable and for how long. This proposed agreement has not yet been approved, and the details of the specific terms (such as the number of affordable units or rent levels) are not provided in the agenda item. If passed, this bylaw would legally bind the owner of 1525 Robson Street to follow the terms of the housing agreement.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, this housing agreement will create legal obligations for the property owner.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 15.	A By-law to enact a Housing Agreement for 1525 Robson Street

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $104M for road and sidewalk repairs
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council approved a $104 million investment over four years (2027–2031) to repair arterial roads, local roads, and sidewalks. This is a major increase from the $34 million in the previous capital plan. Council also directed staff to report back with performance targets, lifecycle cost analyses, and backlog reduction metrics to show how this funding will improve road conditions and support long-term asset performance beyond 2031.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required. Monitor City reports for performance targets and any future road work schedules that may affect your business access.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT Council direct staff to include in the 2027–2031 Capital Plan funding at the “Maximum Delivery Capacity” scenario identified in the Weekly Council Q&A, in the approximate amount of $104 million over four years, to restore the City’s arterial road, local road, and sidewalk networks toward a state of good repair, representing a significant increase from the $34 million allocated in the 2023–2026 Capital Plan for renewal of these assets; FURTHER THAT staff report back with proposed performance targets for arterial roads, local roads, and sidewalks, including lifecycle cost analyses and backlog reduction metrics, outlining how this investment will improve overall network condition and support sustained asset performance beyond 2031. carried

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved seven BIA renewals for 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council approved the renewal of seven Business Improvement Areas (BIAs) for 2026. A BIA is a group of local businesses that work together to improve their neighbourhood, like cleaning streets, running events, or marketing the area. The approved BIAs are: Dunbar Village, Hastings Crossing, Kerrisdale, Kitsilano 4th Avenue, Mount Pleasant, West Broadway, and Davie Village (West End). Council also directed city lawyers to write new bylaws to re-establish each BIA, set new funding limits, and collect money for business promotion programs. This decision was made on consent, meaning it passed without debate.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you own a business in one of these seven BIAs, check with your BIA association for any changes to fees or programs starting in 2026.
minutes_briefs.source_body: A. THAT Council approve the application of the Dunbar Village Business Association as described in the Report dated February 3, 2026, entitled “Approval of Business Improvement Areas (BIAs) Renewals 2026”; FURTHER THAT Council instruct the Director of Legal Services, in consultation with the Senior Director of Business and Economy Office, to bring forward by-laws to re-establish the Dunbar Village BIA, to establish a new funding ceiling, and to remit monies for the business promotion scheme. B. THAT Council approve the application of the Hastings Crossing Business Improvement Association as described in the Report dated February 3, 2026, entitled “Approval of Business Improvement Areas (BIAs) Renewals 2026”; FURTHER THAT Council instruct the Director of Legal Services, in consultation with the Senior Director of Business and Economy Office, to bring forward by-laws to re-establish the Hastings Crossing BIA, to establish a new funding ceiling, and to remit monies for the business promotion scheme. C. THAT Council approve the application of the Kerrisdale Business Association as described in the Report dated February 3, 2026, entitled “Approval of Business Improvement Areas (BIAs) Renewals 2026”; FURTHER THAT Council instruct the Director of Legal Services, in consultation with the Senior Director of Business and Economy Office, to bring forward by-laws to re-establish the Kerrisdale BIA, to establish a new funding ceiling, and to remit monies for the business promotion scheme. D. THAT Council approve the application of the Kitsilano 4th Avenue Business Association as described in the Report dated February 3, 2026, entitled “Approval of Business Improvement Areas (BIAs) Renewals 2026”; FURTHER THAT Council instruct the Director of Legal Services, in consultation with the Senior Director of Business and Economy Office, to bring forward by-laws to re-establish the Kitsilano 4th Avenue BIA, to establish a new funding ceiling, and to remit monies for the business promotion scheme. E. THAT Council approve the application of the Mount Pleasant Commercial Improvement Society as described in the Report dated February 3, 2026, entitled “Approval of Business Improvement Areas (BIAs) Renewals 2026”; FURTHER THAT Council instruct the Director of Legal Services, in consultation with the Senior Director of Business and Economy Office, to bring forward by-laws to re-establish and consolidate the Mount Pleasant BIAs, to establish a new funding ceiling, and to remit monies for the business promotion scheme. F. THAT Council approve the application of the West Broadway Business Improvement Association as described in the Report dated February 3, 2026, entitled “Approval of Business Improvement Areas (BIAs) Renewals 2026”; FURTHER THAT Council instruct the Director of Legal Services, in consultation with the Senior Director of Business and Economy Office, to bring forward by-laws to re-establish the West Broadway BIA, to establish a new funding ceiling, and to remit monies for the business promotion scheme. G. THAT Council approve the application of the Davie Village Business Improvement Association as described in the Report dated February 3, 2026, entitled “Approval of Business Improvement Areas (BIAs) Renewals 2026”; FURTHER THAT Council instruct the Director of Legal Services, in consultation with the Senior Director of Business and Economy Office, to bring forward by-laws to re-establish the West End BIA, to establish a new funding ceiling, and to remit monies for the business promotion scheme. ADOPTED ON CONSENT (Vote No. 11408) (Councillor Bligh absent for the vote)

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved road closure and sale for $280,000
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver Council voted to close and sell a 616-square-foot portion of road next to 1710-1730 East Pender Street to the adjacent property owner. The sale price is $280,000. The money from the sale will go into the city's Capital Fund. This decision is final.
minutes_briefs.source_body: A. THAT Council close, stop-up and convey to the owner of 1710-1730 East Pender Street (the “Abutting Lands” as described in Appendix B of the Report dated January 23, 2026, entitled “Closure and Sale of a Portion of Road Adjacent to 1710-1730 East Pender Street”) that approximately 57.2 square metre (616 square foot) portion of road legally described as PID: 016-709-951; The North 7 Feet of Lot 4 and PID:015-153-258; The North 7 Feet of Lot 5; both of Lot 6, Block D, District Lot 183, Plan 729 (hereinafter, the “Road Portion”), the same as generally shown hatched on the plan attached as Appendix A of the above-noted report, subject to the terms and conditions noted in Appendix B in the same report. B. THAT the sale proceeds of $280,000 be credited to the Capital Fund. ADOPTED ON CONSENT (Vote No. 11410) (Councillor Bligh absent for the vote)

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved new street names for Marpole and St. Paul's developments
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has passed a bylaw to officially name several new street extensions in the Marpole Transit Centre development and the New St. Paul's Hospital development. The bylaw amends the existing Street Name By-law No. 4054 to add these new street names. This decision finalizes the naming process for these streets, which are part of major development projects in the city.
minutes_briefs.source_body: 4. A By-law to amend Street Name By-law No. 4054 regarding the naming of various street extensions in the Marpole Transit Centre Development and New St. Paul’s Hospital Development (By-law No. 14614)

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved FIFA-accredited vehicles in Vancouver special use lanes
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has approved a decision to allow FIFA-accredited vehicles to travel in special use lanes (like bus lanes) from June 11 to July 19, 2026. These vehicles cannot stop in those lanes. Council also asked the Mayor to write to the provincial government for permission to extend this rule to lanes within 800 metres of a highway. This was done to help with traffic during the FIFA World Cup events.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required. If your business operates vehicles that may qualify for FIFA accreditation, prepare to apply for accreditation before June 11, 2026.

key_dates.label: FIFA-accredited vehicle lane access period begins
FIFA-accredited vehicle lane access period ends

minutes_briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Hotel & Accommodation, Professional Services
minutes_briefs.source_body: A. THAT, pursuant to section 124.2 (1) of the Motor Vehicle Act, Council expressly permit FIFA-accredited vehicles to travel but not stop in special use lanes in the City of Vancouver for the duration of the period from June 11 to July 19, 2026. B. THAT, pursuant to Section 124.2 (2), Council request that the Mayor write to the provincial Minister of Transportation and Transit seeking authorization to allow FIFA-accredited vehicles to travel, but not stop, in designated special use lanes located within 800 metres of an arterial highway, for the period from June 11 to July 19, 2026. ADOPTED ON CONSENT (Vote No. 11411) (Councillor Bligh absent for the vote)

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved land use change for former Molson Brewery site
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver Council has approved a land use change for the former Molson Brewery site at 1550 Burrard Street and 1655 West 1st Avenue. The site will be redesignated from "Industrial and Employment" to "Mixed-Use High-Rise 2" under the Vancouver Official Development Plan (ODP). Council also directed staff to begin talks with Metro Vancouver to change the site's regional designation from "Industrial" to "General Urban". This decision does not create any legal rights for the property owner or obligate the City to approve any future development. A public hearing and further approvals are still needed before any bylaw changes are final.
minutes_briefs.source_body: A. THAT, subject to Council approval and enactment of the Vancouver Official Development Plan By-law (“Vancouver ODP”), Council directs the General Manager of Planning, Urban Design and Sustainability to: i. bring forward an application to amend the Vancouver ODP to redesignate the former Molson Brewery site (1550 Burrard Street and 1655 West 1st Avenue) from “Industrial and Employment” to “Mixed-Use High-Rise 2”; and ii. commence consultation on the Vancouver ODP amendment application proposed in (A)(i) above with the following potentially affected persons, organizations, and authorities: Musqueam, Squamish and Tsleil-Waututh Nations, Provincial Government (Ministry of Transportation and Infrastructure), Metro Vancouver Regional District, TransLink, Vancouver School Board and Conseil scolaire francophone, and the general public. B. THAT staff be directed to initiate discussions with Metro Vancouver staff at the earliest opportunity regarding a Type 3 amendment to the Regional Growth Strategy to re-designate the Molson site from “Industrial” to “General Urban”. C. THAT A and B above be adopted on the following conditions: i. THAT the passage of the above resolutions creates no legal rights for the property owner or any other person, or obligation on the part of the City; and any expenditure of funds or incurring of costs is at the risk of the person making the expenditure or incurring the cost; ii. THAT any approval that may be granted following a Public Hearing will not obligate the City to enact a by-law amending an ODP or rezoning the property; and iii. THAT the City and all its officials, including any Approving Officers, will not in any way be limited or directed in the exercise of their authority or discretion regardless of when they are called upon to exercise such authority or discretion. CARRIED (Vote No. 11407) (Councillor Maloney opposed)

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved bylaw amendment for Fire Department appointments
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver Council has passed a bylaw (By-law No. 14617) that amends the existing Fire By-law No. 14419. The change updates the rules around how appointments are made within the Vancouver Fire Department. This is an administrative update to the city's fire regulations.
minutes_briefs.source_body: 7. A By-law to amend Fire By-law No. 14419 regarding appointments (By-law No. 14617)

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved a housing agreement for 1302-1318 E 12th Avenue
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed a bylaw (By-law No. 14618) to enact a Housing Agreement for the property at 1302-1318 E 12th Avenue. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and the property owner that locks in certain conditions, like keeping the units affordable or limiting rent increases, for a set period. This decision is now final and the agreement is in effect.
minutes_briefs.source_body: 8. A By-law to enact a Housing Agreement for 1302 – 1318 E 12th Avenue (By-law No. 14618)

This is a legal contract between the city and the property owner that locks in certain housing conditions, such as how many rental units must be kept affordable and for how long. minutes_briefs.title: Council approved housing agreement amendment for 625 Pacific Street
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed a bylaw to amend the existing housing agreement for properties at 625 Pacific Street, 777 Pacific Street, and 1390 Granville Street (Sub-Area C - 675 Pacific Street and 1395 Rolston Street). This decision finalizes changes to the legal contract that governs how these housing units must be operated, including affordability and rental terms. The amendment was authorized under By-law No. 14622, which updates the original agreement from By-law No. 14225.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved road closure and sale near 1710-1730 East Pender
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver Council voted unanimously to close and sell two small strips of road (each 7 feet wide) next to properties at 1710-1730 East Pender. The land will be transferred to the owner of the neighbouring properties. The owner must then combine this land with their existing lot and dedicate a new road to the City, as shown on a survey plan dated October 12, 2023. The City's Director of Legal Services must approve the final subdivision plan.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you are the owner of 1710-1730 East Pender, contact the City's Director of Legal Services to finalize the subdivision and road dedication.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THERFORE BE IT RESOLVED THAT [PID: 016-709-951] The North 7 Feet of Lot 4 of Lot 6, Block D, District Lot 183, Plan 729, and [PID: 015-153-258] The North 7 Feet of Lot 5 of Lot 6, Block D, District Lot 183, Plan 729; both established as road by document filed DF38229, be closed, stopped-up and conveyed to the owner of the Abutting Lands; FURTHER THAT the said portion of road to be closed is to be subdivided with the Abutting Lands to dedicate road to the City and form a single parcel, as shown in heavy bold outline on the Subdivision Plan prepared by Michael Rinsma, B.C.L.S., completed on the 12th day of October, 2023, re-inspected on the 20th day of January, 2026 and numbered EPP147805, a copy of which is attached hereto, to the satisfaction of the Director of Legal Services. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council rejected amendment to study $104M accessibility funding gap
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver Council voted down a motion that would have asked staff to report back on whether $104 million in capital funding is enough to pay for accessibility and equity measures like sidewalks, curb-cuts, transit routes, and active transport upgrades. The amendment was lost, meaning no further study of the funding gap will be done before the Capital Budget is considered.
minutes_briefs.source_body: AMENDMENT MOVED by Councillor Maloney SECONDED by Councillor Orr THAT the following be added to the end of the motion: AND FURTHER THAT staff report back, prior to consideration of the Capital Budget by Council, on whether the $104 million funding is sufficient to achieve funding of measures that contribute to accessibility and equity, including prioritising sidewalks, best-practice curb-cuts, transit routes and maintenance and upgrades of active transport network to ensure that the expenditure implements the priorities contained in existing City policies on transport priorities. LOST (Vote No. 11404) (Councillors Dominato, Kirby-Yung, Klassen, Meiszner, Montague, Zhou and Mayor Sim opposed)
